name: Strategic Culture Alignment Ontology
alternateName: CULTURE-ONT — Culture-Strategy Alignment Matrix, Observable Signals & Shift Targets
description: Formalizes the relationship between strategy and organisational culture as a measurable, assessable, and actionable domain. Culture is not a separate concern from strategy — it is the execution environment. If the culture contradicts the strategy, the culture wins. This ontology provides the four-quadrant alignment matrix (Aligned, Aspirational, Legacy, Conflicting), observable cultural signal taxonomy, measurable culture shift targets, and quadrant-specific communication response patterns. Each stated strategic value is mapped to observable cultural signals and classified into a quadrant, enabling targeted communication interventions. Part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication).
oaa:domain: Organisational Culture, Strategy Execution, Change Management

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Strategy-to-Culture Assessment
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Traverses from VSOM strategy through stated values to observable cultural signals, classifying each into an alignment quadrant. This is the core culture-strategy diagnostic.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: vsom:VSOMFramework → cul:alignmentAssessesVSOM → cul:CultureStrategyAlignment → cul:hasAssessmentEntry → cul:CultureAssessmentEntry → cul:assessesValue → cul:CultureValue → cul:entryObservesSignal → cul:CulturalSignal
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Diagnose culture-strategy alignment for each stated strategic value

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Quadrant-to-Communication Response
oaa:joinPatterns.description: From each alignment quadrant, select the appropriate communication response pattern. Each quadrant demands a different approach: celebrate (Aligned), bridge (Aspirational), build awareness (Legacy), confront honestly (Conflicting).
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: cul:CultureAssessmentEntry → cul:entryInQuadrant → cul:AlignmentQuadrant → cul:quadrantHasResponse → cul:CommunicationResponse → cul:responseUsesPattern → nar:CommunicationPattern
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Select quadrant-appropriate communication strategy for culture gaps

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Culture Shift Measurement
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Track culture shift targets over time — from current quadrant toward target quadrant — with measurable signal changes as evidence of progress.
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: cul:CultureShiftTarget → cul:shiftTargetForEntry → cul:CultureAssessmentEntry → cul:entryInQuadrant → cul:AlignmentQuadrant [current vs target]
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Measure progress of culture change initiatives

entities.name: CultureStrategyAlignment
entities.description: Container for a complete culture-strategy alignment assessment. Maps stated strategic values against observable cultural signals to produce a quadrant classification for each value. The assessment reveals where culture supports strategy (Aligned), where culture is willing but not yet there (Aspirational), where culture is indifferent (Legacy), and where culture actively contradicts strategy (Conflicting).

oaa:properties.name: vsomFrameworkRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VSOMFramework @id being assessed

oaa:properties.name: contextRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to orgctx:OrganizationContext @id

oaa:properties.name: assessedBy
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id or team who conducted assessment

oaa:properties.name: overallAlignmentScore
oaa:properties.description: Weighted alignment score (0-100). >80 = culturally aligned, <40 = significant culture-strategy gap

oaa:properties.name: quadrantDistribution
oaa:properties.description: Count of values in each quadrant: e.g. 'Aligned:3, Aspirational:2, Legacy:1, Conflicting:1'

oaa:properties.name: criticalConflicts
oaa:properties.description: Values classified as Conflicting that pose highest risk to strategy execution

entities.name: AlignmentQuadrant
entities.description: One of four quadrants in the culture-strategy alignment matrix. Each quadrant represents a different relationship between stated strategy and observable culture, and demands a different communication response. The matrix has two axes: stated strategy (horizontal) and observable culture (vertical).

oaa:properties.name: quadrantType
oaa:properties.description: Matrix quadrant classification

oaa:properties.name: characterisation
oaa:properties.description: How this quadrant manifests in the organisation

oaa:properties.name: riskLevel
oaa:properties.description: Risk to strategy execution

oaa:properties.name: communicationApproach
oaa:properties.description: High-level communication approach for this quadrant

Aligned.characterisation: Culture reinforces strategy — observable behaviours, incentives, and rituals actively support stated strategic values
Aligned.communicationApproach: Celebrate and reinforce — use case studies of strategy-culture alignment

Aspirational.characterisation: Culture says yes but behaviour says not yet — willingness without capability or habit
Aspirational.communicationApproach: Bridge narratives — acknowledge the gap, show the path, celebrate early signals

Legacy.characterisation: Culture doesn't address strategy — not conflicting, just indifferent or unaware
Legacy.communicationApproach: Awareness building — connect new strategy to existing cultural strengths

Conflicting.characterisation: Culture actively contradicts strategy — incentives, behaviours, or rituals work against stated values
Conflicting.communicationApproach: Honest confrontation — name the contradiction, define what must change, set measurable culture shift targets

entities.name: CulturalSignal
entities.description: An observable organisational signal that indicates cultural state. Signals are the evidence base for culture-strategy alignment assessment — they are what people actually do, reward, celebrate, and say, as opposed to what the strategy document declares. Five signal categories: behaviours, incentives, rituals, symbols, and language.

oaa:properties.name: signalCategory
oaa:properties.description: Type of cultural signal

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: What is observed (e.g. 'Managers publicly celebrate compliance over innovation')

oaa:properties.name: observedIn
oaa:properties.description: Where/when this signal is observable (e.g. 'quarterly reviews', 'Slack channels', 'promotion decisions')

oaa:properties.name: frequency
oaa:properties.description: How often this signal is observed

oaa:properties.name: strength
oaa:properties.description: How strongly this signal indicates cultural state

oaa:properties.name: contextRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to orgctx:OrganizationContext @id where observed

oaa:properties.name: evidenceNotes
oaa:properties.description: Specific evidence or examples supporting this signal observation

entities.name: CultureValue
entities.description: A stated strategic value being assessed for cultural alignment. Values are extracted from the VSOM strategy (Vision component) and represent what the organisation says it stands for. Each value is assessed against observable cultural signals to determine alignment quadrant.

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: The stated value (e.g. 'Innovation', 'Customer-First', 'Transparency')

oaa:properties.name: source
oaa:properties.description: Where this value is stated (e.g. 'VSOM Vision Component', 'Corporate Strategy Document')

oaa:properties.name: vsomVisionRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to vsom:VisionComponent @id if applicable

oaa:properties.name: strategicImportance
oaa:properties.description: How important this value is to strategy execution

oaa:properties.name: bscPerspectiveAlignment
oaa:properties.description: Which BSC perspective this value most closely aligns to

entities.name: CultureAssessmentEntry
entities.description: Individual mapping of a stated strategic value to observed cultural signals, resulting in a quadrant classification. This is the core unit of analysis: 'For value X, we observe signals Y, which places it in quadrant Z.' The entry includes both supporting signals (evidence of alignment) and contradicting signals (evidence of misalignment).

oaa:properties.name: valueRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to cul:CultureValue @id being assessed

oaa:properties.name: quadrantRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to cul:AlignmentQuadrant @id classification result

oaa:properties.name: supportingSignalRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to cul:CulturalSignal @ids that support alignment

oaa:properties.name: contradictingSignalRefs
oaa:properties.description: References to cul:CulturalSignal @ids that contradict alignment

oaa:properties.name: alignmentScore
oaa:properties.description: Alignment score for this specific value (0-100)

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Confidence in the quadrant classification based on signal strength and coverage

oaa:properties.name: commentary
oaa:properties.description: Qualitative assessment of the alignment state for this value

entities.name: CultureShiftTarget
entities.description: A measurable target for culture change linked to a specific value and quadrant. Defines what 'good' looks like: which signals should increase, which should decrease, and by when. Culture shift targets are reviewed at each VSOM review cycle — culture change is measured, not hoped for.

oaa:properties.name: assessmentEntryRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to cul:CultureAssessmentEntry @id this target addresses

oaa:properties.name: targetQuadrant
oaa:properties.description: Target quadrant (always Aligned or Aspirational — never Legacy or Conflicting)

oaa:properties.name: signalsToIncrease
oaa:properties.description: Cultural signals that should become more frequent/stronger

oaa:properties.name: signalsToDecrease
oaa:properties.description: Cultural signals that should become less frequent/weaker

oaa:properties.name: measurementMethod
oaa:properties.description: How progress will be measured (e.g. 'engagement survey', 'behaviour observation', 'incentive audit')

oaa:properties.name: ownerRoleRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to rrr:ExecutiveRole @id accountable for culture shift

entities.name: CommunicationResponse
entities.description: A quadrant-specific communication approach for addressing culture-strategy alignment (or misalignment). Each quadrant has a fundamentally different communication need: Aligned values need celebration, Aspirational values need bridge narratives, Legacy values need awareness building, Conflicting values need honest confrontation. The response references a nar:CommunicationPattern for delivery.

oaa:properties.name: quadrantRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to cul:AlignmentQuadrant @id this response addresses

oaa:properties.name: responseStrategy
oaa:properties.description: High-level response approach (e.g. 'Celebrate and reinforce', 'Name the contradiction')

oaa:properties.name: patternRef
oaa:properties.description: Reference to nar:CommunicationPattern @id recommended for this response

oaa:properties.name: keyMessages
oaa:properties.description: Core messages for this quadrant response

oaa:properties.name: avoidances
oaa:properties.description: What NOT to communicate (e.g. 'Don't blame individuals for systemic culture issues')

oaa:properties.name: successIndicators
oaa:properties.description: Observable signals that the communication response is working

relationships.name: Alignment Assesses VSOM
relationships.description: Culture-strategy alignment assessment evaluates alignment with a VSOM strategy

relationships.name: Alignment In Context
relationships.description: Assessment conducted within an organisational context

relationships.name: Has Assessment Entry
relationships.description: Alignment assessment contains individual value-to-signal-to-quadrant entries

relationships.name: Assesses Value
relationships.description: Assessment entry evaluates a specific stated strategic value

relationships.name: Entry In Quadrant
relationships.description: Assessment entry classified into an alignment quadrant

relationships.name: Entry Observes Signal
relationships.description: Assessment entry references observed cultural signals as evidence

relationships.name: Signal Observed In Context
relationships.description: Cultural signal observed within an organisational context

relationships.name: Quadrant Has Response
relationships.description: Each alignment quadrant has a recommended communication response

relationships.name: Response Uses Pattern
relationships.description: Communication response uses a narrative communication pattern for delivery

relationships.name: Shift Target For Entry
relationships.description: Culture shift target addresses a specific assessment entry's quadrant gap

relationships.name: Shift Owned By Role
relationships.description: Culture shift target is accountable to an executive role

businessRules.name: Assessment Must Reference VSOM
businessRules.action: THEN vsomFrameworkRef MUST reference a valid vsom:VSOMFramework @id

businessRules.name: Assessment Entry Must Have Signals
businessRules.action: THEN at least one of supportingSignalRefs or contradictingSignalRefs MUST be non-empty — classification must be evidence-based

businessRules.name: Conflicting Values Must Have Shift Target
businessRules.action: THEN a CultureShiftTarget SHOULD be created to address the conflict

businessRules.name: Shift Target Must Aim Higher
businessRules.action: THEN targetQuadrant MUST be 'Aligned' or 'Aspirational' — culture shifts always aim toward alignment

changeControl.changeNotes: Initial release of CULTURE-ONT as part of VE-Series VSOM-SC (Strategy Communication). Formalizes culture-strategy alignment assessment with four-quadrant matrix, cultural signal taxonomy, shift targets, and communication responses. Cross-references VSOM-ONT v3.0.0, NARRATIVE-ONT v1.0.0, RRR-ONT v4.0.0, ORG-CONTEXT-ONT v3.0.0, BSC-ONT v1.0.0.

competencyQuestions.question: What is the role and purpose of CultureStrategyAlignment within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of AlignmentQuadrant within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CulturalSignal within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CultureValue within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CultureAssessmentEntry within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CultureShiftTarget within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of CommunicationResponse within this ontology?
